---
title: "Decision tree: which method?"
toc: true
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.kicker}
APPENDIX · DECISION TREE
:::
# Decision tree: which method?
::: {.lead}
A branching guide from what you are asking to the lab that shows you
how to answer it.
:::
:::

The tree below is not a substitute for thinking; it is a scaffold for
thinking. Start from your scientific question, follow the branches, and
land on a lab. Every leaf links into the curriculum.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  Start{What is the question?}
  Start --> Q1[Compare two or more groups]
  Start --> Q2[Quantify an association]
  Start --> Q3[Model an outcome from predictors]
  Start --> Q4[Handle clustered or repeated data]
  Start --> Q5[Deal with high-dimensional data]
  Start --> Q6[Estimate a causal effect]

  Q1 --> G1[Continuous outcome]
  G1 --> G1a[Two groups → t-test / Wilcoxon]
  G1 --> G1b[Many groups → ANOVA / Kruskal-Wallis]

  Q1 --> G2[Binary outcome]
  G2 --> G2a[Two proportions → chi-square / Fisher]
  G2 --> G2b[Risk or odds ratio]

  Q2 --> A1[Two continuous → Pearson / Spearman]
  Q2 --> A2[Continuous & categorical → ANOVA]
  Q2 --> A3[Categorical & categorical → chi-square]

  Q3 --> M1[Continuous outcome → linear regression]
  Q3 --> M2[Binary → logistic]
  Q3 --> M3[Count → Poisson / NB]
  Q3 --> M4[Ordinal → ordinal logit]
  Q3 --> M5[Time-to-event → Cox / KM]

  Q4 --> C1[Linear mixed model]
  Q4 --> C2[GLMM or GEE]
  Q4 --> C3[Repeated measures ANOVA]

  Q5 --> H1[Regularisation → lasso / ridge]
  Q5 --> H2[Dimension reduction → PCA / UMAP]
  Q5 --> H3[Tree ensemble → RF / xgboost]

  Q6 --> CA1[Randomised → ITT analysis]
  Q6 --> CA2[Observational → DAG → adjustment set]
  Q6 --> CA3[Non-randomised → IPTW / matching]
  Q6 --> CA4[Strong instrument → IV]
```

## Using the tree

The first branch is always about the shape of your question: *comparison*,
*association*, *modelling*, *clustering/repetition*, *dimension*, or
*causation*. The later branches are about the shape of your data:
*continuous*, *binary*, *count*, *time-to-event*.

## Comparing groups

- **Two groups, continuous:** start with a t-test if normality holds, the
  Wilcoxon rank-sum otherwise. See [Course 1 W4 S1](../course1_foundations/labs/lab_week4_session1.qmd) and [W4 S4](../course1_foundations/labs/lab_week4_session4.qmd).
- **More than two groups, continuous:** ANOVA (or Kruskal-Wallis). See
  [Course 2 W2 S1](../course2_regression/labs/lab_week2_session1.qmd).
- **Two proportions:** chi-square with large cells, Fisher with small.
  See [Course 1 W4 S2](../course1_foundations/labs/lab_week4_session2.qmd).

## Associations

- **Two continuous variables:** Pearson if roughly linear and normal,
  Spearman otherwise. See [Course 1 W4 S3](../course1_foundations/labs/lab_week4_session3.qmd).
- **A continuous and a categorical variable:** ANOVA is equivalent to a
  linear model with a categorical predictor. See [Course 2 W1 S2](../course2_regression/labs/lab_week1_session2.qmd).
- **Two categorical variables:** chi-square test of independence, or
  loglinear model for more than two factors.

## Regression modelling

- **Continuous outcome → linear regression.** [Course 2 W1](../course2_regression/labs/lab_week1_session2.qmd).
- **Binary outcome → logistic regression.** [Course 2 W3 S1](../course2_regression/labs/lab_week3_session1.qmd).
- **Count outcome → Poisson / negative binomial.** [Course 2 W3 S4](../course2_regression/labs/lab_week3_session4.qmd).
- **Ordinal outcome → ordinal logit.** [Course 2 W3 S3](../course2_regression/labs/lab_week3_session3.qmd).
- **Time-to-event → Cox proportional hazards.** [Course 2 W4 S3](../course2_regression/labs/lab_week4_session3.qmd).

## Clustered or repeated data

- **Continuous outcome:** linear mixed model. [Course 3 W2 S3](../course3_design_causal/labs/lab_week2_session3.qmd).
- **Binary or count outcome:** GLMM or GEE. [Course 3 W2 S4](../course3_design_causal/labs/lab_week2_session4.qmd).

## High-dimensional data

- **Many predictors:** regularisation with `glmnet`. [Course 4 W1 S2](../course4_ml_highdim/labs/lab_week1_session2.qmd).
- **Many variables, fewer dimensions wanted:** PCA, UMAP, t-SNE. [Course 4 W1 S3](../course4_ml_highdim/labs/lab_week1_session3.qmd) and [S5](../course4_ml_highdim/labs/lab_week1_session5.qmd).
- **Prediction on messy tabular data:** tree ensembles. [Course 4 W2 S1](../course4_ml_highdim/labs/lab_week2_session1.qmd).

## Estimating a causal effect

- **Randomised trial:** analyse by ITT. [Course 3 W1 S2](../course3_design_causal/labs/lab_week1_session2.qmd).
- **Observational data:** draw a DAG, pick an adjustment set.
  [Course 3 W3 S3](../course3_design_causal/labs/lab_week3_session3.qmd).
- **Non-randomised treatment:** propensity scores or IPTW. [Course 3 W3 S4](../course3_design_causal/labs/lab_week3_session4.qmd).
- **A plausible instrument:** instrumental-variable regression. [Course 3 W3 S5](../course3_design_causal/labs/lab_week3_session5.qmd).
